title: Human Rights Act 1998
text:
Title: Human Rights Act 1998
Citation: Human Rights Act 1998
Jurisdiction: uk
Source URL: https://www.legislation.gov.uk/ukpga/1998/42/contents

The Human Rights Act incorporates Convention rights into UK domestic law. Public authorities must act compatibly with Convention rights, courts must interpret legislation compatibly so far as possible, and higher courts may issue declarations of incompatibility without invalidating primary legislation.

title: Constitutional Reform Act 2005
text:
Title: Constitutional Reform Act 2005
Citation: Constitutional Reform Act 2005
Jurisdiction: uk
Source URL: https://www.legislation.gov.uk/ukpga/2005/4/contents

This Act altered the office of Lord Chancellor, strengthened judicial independence, created the Supreme Court of the United Kingdom, and established the Judicial Appointments Commission. It is central to UK separation of powers and judicial independence research.

title: European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018
text:
Title: European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018
Citation: European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018
Jurisdiction: uk
Source URL: https://www.legislation.gov.uk/ukpga/2018/16/contents

The Withdrawal Act repealed the European Communities Act 1972 and retained much EU-derived law as domestic law after exit day. It is important for questions of retained EU law, delegated powers, interpretive continuity, supremacy, and post-Brexit constitutional change.

title: Constitutional Reform and Governance Act 2010
text:
Title: Constitutional Reform and Governance Act 2010
Citation: Constitutional Reform and Governance Act 2010
Jurisdiction: uk
Source URL: https://www.legislation.gov.uk/ukpga/2010/25/contents

CRAG 2010 places treaty ratification before Parliament under a statutory laying procedure and reforms civil service and public records rules. It is frequently relevant to treaty-making, prerogative powers, and parliamentary control of international agreements.

title: Equality Act 2010
text:
Title: Equality Act 2010
Citation: Equality Act 2010
Jurisdiction: uk
Source URL: https://www.legislation.gov.uk/ukpga/2010/15/contents

The Equality Act consolidates UK anti-discrimination law. It protects characteristics including age, disability, gender reassignment, marriage and civil partnership, pregnancy and maternity, race, religion or belief, sex, and sexual orientation, and structures direct discrimination, indirect discrimination, harassment, victimisation, and public sector equality duties.

title: Data Protection Act 2018
text:
Title: Data Protection Act 2018
Citation: Data Protection Act 2018
Jurisdiction: uk
Source URL: https://www.legislation.gov.uk/ukpga/2018/12/contents

The Data Protection Act 2018 supplements UK data protection rules and implements law-enforcement and intelligence-processing regimes. It is read with the UK GDPR and is relevant to privacy, processing bases, data subject rights, enforcement, and public authority processing.

title: Police and Criminal Evidence Act 1984
text:
Title: Police and Criminal Evidence Act 1984
Citation: Police and Criminal Evidence Act 1984
Jurisdiction: uk
Source URL: https://www.legislation.gov.uk/ukpga/1984/60/contents

PACE is the core UK statute on stop and search, arrest, detention, interview, custody records, search warrants, seizure, and codes of practice. For practical criminal-procedure research it is the first source for arrest powers, detention safeguards, access to legal advice, treatment of detainees, and the structure of police powers in England and Wales.

title: Road Traffic Act 1988
text:
Title: Road Traffic Act 1988
Citation: Road Traffic Act 1988
Jurisdiction: uk
Source URL: https://www.legislation.gov.uk/ukpga/1988/52/contents

The Road Traffic Act 1988 regulates driver licensing, driving offences, document production, disqualification, insurance, and police powers connected to road traffic enforcement. It is central to questions about driving entitlement, roadside production of licences, penalties for unlicensed driving, and the interaction between traffic offences and criminal procedure.
